macro_summary: U.S. equities edged sideways ahead of the June payrolls report, with the S&P 500 closing at 745.76 (+0.11%) and the Nasdaq 100 up 0.11%. Meta's plan to launch an AI cloud business (per Bloomberg) weighed on infrastructure names early, but the selling was largely absorbed. Oil continued to leak lower as Hormuz exports recovered above 10 mb/d and indirect U.S.-Iran talks advanced (per World Oil). Fed Chair Warsh reiterated the 2% inflation target at the ECB forum, keeping a September rate hike on the table.
commodities_note: WTI slipped to $68.08 (+0.55% on the day, but -27.4% in a month) as the Hormuz risk premium unwound further. Gold was steady at $4,077. The 10-year Treasury yield rose 1 bp to 4.47% ahead of the jobs data.

accumulate_gates.note: All mechanical gates PASS, but momentum_warn is True (weak recent tape). ACCUMULATE requires either an explicit multi-year structural support cite (200-day SMA / prior cycle low / named floor) OR an imminent hard catalyst within 7 days (specific date). Generic fundamental praise does NOT clear the bar — default to WATCH if neither anchor applies.

writeup.headline: Upgraded to ACCUMULATE — technical reset yields 2.2:1 sizing R:R, Blackwell thesis intact.
writeup.what_to_do: Entry zone $194.50–$197.58, invalidation below $190.73 (200-day SMA). Headline R:R 4.9:1 is distorted by the tight stop; sizing R:R to structural support is a clean 2.2:1. Blackwell Ultra ramp and Vera Rubin volume production provide two independent demand legs. The pre-market -0.9% on the SMCI Taiwan probe (per Reuters, Jul 2) is noise — the enforcement action targets Super Micro, not Nvidia. Half position due to single-architecture concentration in the near term. ACCUMULATE -- starter position. Fragility: Blackwell dominates near-term revenue — second leg (Rubin) verifies the durability. [paper]

singapore.key_development: US10Y above 4.47% and MAS' tight S$NEER persist — no relief signal yet for NIM compression.

semiconductors.summary: The semis cluster is deeply bifurcated. NVDA has reset to a 2.2:1 sizing R:R and upgrades to ACCUMULATE, while MU, AMD and ASML remain badly extended and hard-blocked. TSM and SITM sit at WATCH with constructive setups. SOXX was essentially flat on the day as the Meta AI cloud headline added noise but didn't break the structural demand story.
semiconductors.key_development: NVDA upgrades to ACCUMULATE on technical reset; extended names need pullbacks to become actionable.

big_tech.summary: BigTech is broadly cautious. MSFT's accumulate condition broke on R:R compression, PLTR is momentum-blocked, and AMZN/GOOG remain below their 50-day averages. Meta's entry into AI cloud services (per Bloomberg) raises the competitive stakes, but the capex-sustainability debate is the real weight. The NFP release is the next catalyst.
big_tech.key_development: Meta's AI cloud ambitions add a competitive overhang for AWS/Azure/Google Cloud, though still early-stage.

interconnected.name: Marvell Technology
interconnected.reason: Custom ASIC designer competing with AVGO — Amazon's reported plan to sell Trainium externally (per Simply Wall St., Jul 2) expands MRVL's addressable market.
interconnected.entry_note: Add to watchlist if Amazon confirms Trainium external sales, driving a re-rating of Marvell's custom ASIC pipeline.

interconnected.name: Coherent
interconnected.reason: NVDA's $2B photonics partner alongside LITE — any NVDA datacenter capex tailwind lifts COHR.
interconnected.entry_note: Becomes interesting on a pullback toward the 200-day SMA if the photonics demand signal from NVDA stays intact.

interconnected.name: Arista Networks
interconnected.reason: Arista is the primary switching vendor for hyperscaler AI clusters — direct beneficiary of NVDA Blackwell ramp and AVGO ASIC deployments.
interconnected.entry_note: Watch for entry on a 50-day SMA reclaim; next earnings could validate AI switching growth.

active_risks: US-Iran Hormuz crisis de-escalating as exports recover above 10 mb/d and indirect talks progress; oil premium unwinding.
Trump declines to renew USMCA, reverting to pre-2020 tariff framework — North American trade uncertainty elevated.
Fed Chair Warsh doubles down on 2% inflation target, keeping a September rate hike on the table if jobs data stays hot.
US-China tech decoupling continues with potential expansion of the Chinese entity blacklist; Pentagon AI investment cited as driver.
geopolitical.market_impact: Hormuz easing has crushed WTI 27% in a month, relieving energy inflation pressure and supporting a low-VIX environment (16.8). However, the Fed's hawkish lean keeps tech multiples capped — GOOG and AMZN remain below their 50-day averages. NVDA's pullback to support offers a rare entry in a sector still dominated by caution.
geopolitical.new_since_yesterday: Trump announced termination of USMCA; Meta formally confirmed AI cloud infrastructure push; NFP on deck Thursday.

base.description: Hormuz stalemate cools, WTI settles near $68; semis stabilise but remain range-bound as rate fears cap upside. NVDA entry remains the best risk/reward.

optimistic.description: US-Iran deal seals oil below $60 by Q3; inflation falls, Fed signals rate cut. Semis and BigTech rally sharply — AVGO and GOOG re-buy.

pessimistic.description: Hormuz reignites on failed talks; WTI jumps above $85, VIX >25, Fed forced to hike. Semis sell off, NVDA entry invalidated.

wildcard.description: China halts rare earth exports to the US, disrupting ASML/TSM supply chains. Tech sector plunges, defense names (PLTR) spike.

geopolitical.portfolio_action: Enter NVDA on the current setup with a starter position; avoid extended semis. No other new entries until NFP clears.

portfolio_snapshot.risk_posture: 6 of 29 names are hard-blocked on technical extension; the dominant risk is a Fed rate hike that could extend the current tech consolidation.

scheduled_tech_events.notes: AI kit, on-device model integration, Vision Pro 2 rumours. Competitive signal for MSFT Copilot and GOOG Gemini on-device.

watch_for_entry.note: Pullback toward the 50-day at $417.49 is the entry zone. R:R 1.2:1 to $476; earnings Jul 16 the catalyst. Half position on a successful 50-day test.

watch_for_entry.note: Within 3% of the 50-day; R:R 3.6:1 to $769. Wide-stop R:R 1.4:1 is tight. Watch for a volume-backed break above $769; 50-day reclaim is the trigger.

regime_change_pending.note: Oracle Project Jupiter gap consolidating — session 19 of 25. Graduates on Path A (SMA50 retest at ~$278) or Path B breakout above $293.06. No entry until graduation.

macro_trigger_map.bullish_outcome: Payrolls below 150k, wages soft — signaling Fed can hold. Tech relief rally.
macro_trigger_map.bearish_outcome: Payrolls >250k, wages surge — cementing a September rate hike. Semis sell off further.

macro_trigger_map.bullish_outcome: CPI below 4.0% YoY, cooling inflation — rate hike off table, tech rallies.
macro_trigger_map.bearish_outcome: CPI above 4.5%, reinforcing inflation peak fears — broad sell-off.
bearish_impact: NVDA entry invalidated if support breaks

accumulate_paper_status.line: ACCUMULATE — paper phase: 8/10 matured, -0.7% alpha, trend_up. Not yet validated; size as paper.

macro_context_line: Rate backdrop (FRED, not live): payrolls +172k (eased from +179k); unemployment 4.3% (flat, stale); last CPI 4.3% YoY (up, stale). Reconcile the rate lean in the macro_summary against this when a CPI/FOMC/jobs event is near.

taxonomy_discrimination.note: Output-only meta-analysis of signal-class discrimination. NOT injected into the analysis prompt and NOT persisted into memory.lessons_learned. full_corpus pools pre-cutover (different-instrument) rows — informational only.

calibration_insights.confidence_banner: Actionable buckets BUY (n=3) / ACCUMULATE (n=4) spanning trend_up. NOT yet decision-grade — alpha figures are benchmark-relative; single-regime samples carry no out-of-regime evidence.
calibration_insights.note: Block is output-only — assembled post-LLM from signal_evaluation_log outcomes and never injected into the analysis prompt. The underlying lesson stats are separately persisted into report_memory.json under lessons_learned and DO reach the next day's prompt via the memory Layer 3 calibration loop. Treat as a reader-facing self-knowledge summary.
